pattern: Sort the distinct values to establish their relative ordering, assign consecutive ranks starting at 1, then map each original element to its precomputed rank.
hints: The rank of an element depends only on how many distinct values are smaller than it. Sorting a deduplicated copy of the array reveals that order.
Build a mapping from each unique value to its rank (1-indexed position in the sorted distinct list), then apply the mapping to every element of the original array.
Use a dictionary: after sorting and deduplicating, enumerate the sorted values and record rank = index + 1. A single pass over the original array then produces the answer.

edge_inputs.description: duplicates interspersed — same value must get same rank

stress.description: 100k integers with many duplicates over a small value range
